name: Battle Creek Christian Early Learning Center
description: Battle Creek Christian Early Learning Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Battle Creek, MI. There are 2 indoor courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. A one-time fee is required to play. Players have access to restrooms.

name: Battle Creek Family YMCA
description: Come play pickleball at Battle Creek Family YMCA in Battle Creek, MI! There are 3 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A membership is required to play. Players have access to lights.

name: Full Blast
description: Full Blast is a place to play pickleball in Battle Creek, MI. There are 9 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Players have access to restrooms, lights, water, and locker rooms. The courts are free.

name: Kellogg Community College
description: Battle Creek Pickleball Club will move play indoors starting in October at FullBlast!

name: Marshall Area Pickleball
description: Marshall Area Pickleball is a place to play pickleball in Marshall, MI. There are 2 outdoor acrylic courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The courts are free.

name: Marshall High School
description: Marshall High School is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Marshall, MI. There are 3 outdoor hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. Amenities include restrooms. The courts are free.

name: Minges Creek Athletic Club
description: Minges Creek Athletic Club is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Battle Creek, MI. There are 5 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Players have access to lights and restrooms. Reservations are available. A membership is required to play.

name: Wattles Park Men's Club
description: Wattles Park Men's Club is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Battle Creek, MI. There are 9 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play. You can reserve the courts. Players have access to restrooms, water, and lights.
